summary: Hot second set with blistering Bucket>Fire and strong The Other One
blurb: The show delivers a scorching second‑set featuring the fiery Bucket > Fire transition, a powerful The Other One, and an unexpected Terrapin Station. Highlights include Queen Jane Approximately and Stella Blue, while a few numbers like Fire on the Mountain fall flat.
review:
On September 8, 1987 at the Providence Civic Center the Dead gave fans a mixed‑quality performance that still sparkles in several key moments. The first set opened mellow with Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and a relaxed Queen Jane Approximately, but it was the second set that truly ignited the night – a blistering Hell In A Bucket → Fire on the Mountain segue (dubbed “Bucket > Fire”) roared through the house, followed by a powerful The Other One and an unexpected Terrapin Station that caught many listeners off‑guard. The jam into Stella Blue remained emotionally resonant, and Bob’s exuberant closing rave‑up on Turn On Your Lovelight sent the audience home on a high note. While some songs (notably Fire on the Mountain) were described as flat, the overall pacing stayed tight, and Phil’s funky riff opening “The Music Never Stopped” kept the groove rolling. The best available source is the crystal‑clear Miller soundboard capture (gd87-09-08.sbd.miller.20994.sbeok.shnf), which preserves the instrument separation despite a few minor cuts in the drum mix.

For Deadheads seeking the definitive experience of this late‑’80s era, the show offers enough standout moments – Queen Jane Approximately, Looks Like Rain, The Other One, and Stella Blue – to make it a worthwhile listen. The audience recording also holds its own for those who enjoy the raw vibe of the In‑The‑Dark period, but the Miller SBD remains the top choice for clarity and completeness.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings praised the show (Bucket>Fire, The Other One, Terrapin Station, Queen Jane) while one was mixed, noting uneven performance on a few songs. The preponderance of positive language (“blistering,” “powerful,” “solid setlist”) justifies a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard mix with crisp instrument separation; captures the full show despite minor drum cuts, making it the clearest and most complete source.

key_highlights: Blistering Hell In A Bucket → Fire on the Mountain transition (Bucket>Fire) that ignites the second set.
Powerful The Other One performance with strong group interplay.
Unexpected Terrapin Station surprise adding a high‑energy peak late in the show.
Solid soundboard recording quality that preserves detail and balances the band’s dynamics.

band_performance.Jerry: No detailed comments; reviewers did not single out his playing for this show.
band_performance.Phil: Delivered a solid, funky bass line on 'The Music Never Stopped' and stayed locked in during 'The Other One.'
band_performance.Bob: Provided an upbeat, high‑energy finish on 'Turn On Your Lovelight,' driving the show's final momentum.
band_performance.Mickey: Percussion was steady overall; one reviewer noted occasional cuts but no major issues reported.
band_performance.Billy: Percussion was solid throughout, complementing the band's dynamics without notable complaints.
